paradigm_definition: Integrate multiple sources, models, methods, or perspectives into a coherent or explicitly plural whole with managed conflict and provenance.

dimension_views.label: Integration Logic
dimension_views.definition: How separate sources, models, methods, or stages are integrated.

selected_values_expanded.label: Shared-space projection
selected_values_expanded.definition: align by projecting inputs into a common space

selected_values_expanded.label: Reconcile
selected_values_expanded.definition: Use fusion to reconcile conflicting or inconsistent sources into a coherent result, while preserving uncertainty and provenance where disagreement remains.

dimension_views.label: Representation Layer
dimension_views.definition: Fusion at the level of encoded representations or formal models.

selected_values_expanded.label: Representation
selected_values_expanded.definition: Fuse encoded or derived representations, e.g., features, embeddings, or latent states.

support_edge_hints.operator_family: Align normalize and semantically map into a shared space
support_edge_hints.mechanism_summary: Figure-2 recurrent interaction pattern: align normalize and semantically map into a shared space. Use when transformation supports fusion through this recurrent interaction pattern.

support_edge_hints.operator_family: Rule based gating or arbitration to manage conflicts
support_edge_hints.mechanism_summary: Figure-2 recurrent interaction pattern: rule based gating or arbitration to manage conflicts. Use when rule supports fusion through this recurrent interaction pattern.
